ingredients.prep: 14-15 oz (or use another can of diced)

steps.text: Season chicken: Pat chicken dry. Season with salt, black pepper, and paprika. If using bone-in/skin-on, season both sides.

Brown chicken: Heat oil in a heavy skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add chicken (skin-side down if applicable) and brown well. Flip and brown the other side lightly. Transfer chicken to a plate (it will finish cooking in the sauce).
Sweat the trinity: Reduce heat to medium. Add butter, then onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened, scraping up browned bits. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Bloom tomato paste: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Build the sauce: Add diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, chicken stock, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, cayenne, Creole seasoning, Worcestershire, and optional hot sauce. Stir and bring to a gentle simmer.

Braise chicken: Return chicken (and any juices) to the pot, nestling pieces into the sauce. Cover partially and simmer until chicken is fully cooked and tender. If bone-in, cook until it pulls easily from the bone.
Finish: Remove bay leaves. Stir in lemon juice, most of the green onions, and most of the parsley. Taste and adjust salt, heat, and seasoning.
Serve: Spoon chicken and Creole tomato gravy over hot cooked rice. Garnish with remaining green onions and parsley.
